title: Multi-Tenant App Access Anomalies
spl:
index=azure sourcetype="azure:aad:signin"
| where crossTenantAccessType IN ("b2bCollaboration","passthrough") AND resourceTenantId!=homeTenantId
| stats count by userPrincipalName, appDisplayName, resourceTenantId
| where count > 10
| sort -count
description: Unexpected tenants or guest users accessing multi-tenant apps may indicate consent phishing or lateral SaaS movement.
value: Unexpected tenants or guest users accessing multi-tenant apps may indicate consent phishing or lateral SaaS movement.
implementation: Baseline B2B access patterns. Alert on new resource tenants for crown-jewel apps. Correlate with consent events (UC-9.3.12).
visualization: Table (cross-tenant access), Heatmap (user √ó tenant), Line chart (volume).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Planned change windows, maintenance, approved automation, and known good service accounts; correlate with change tickets and identity team communication.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Entra sign-in logs (`resourceTenantId`, `crossTenantAccessType`, `homeTenantId`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Baseline B2B access patterns. Alert on new resource tenants for crown-jewel apps. Correlate with consent events (UC-9.3.12).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=azure sourcetype="azure:aad:signin"
| where crossTenantAccessType IN ("b2bCollaboration","passthrough") AND resourceTenantId!=homeTenantId
| stats count by userPrincipalName, appDisplayName, resourceTenantId
| where count > 10
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Multi-Tenant App Access Anomalies** ‚Äî Unexpected tenants or guest users accessing multi-tenant apps may indicate consent phishing or lateral SaaS movement.

Documented **Data sources**: Entra sign-in logs (`resourceTenantId`, `crossTenantAccessType`, `homeTenantId`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: azure; **sourcetype**: azure:aad:signin. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=azure, sourcetype="azure:aad:signin". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where crossTenantAccessType IN ("b2bCollaboration","passthrough") AND resourceTenantId!=homeTenantId` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by userPrincipalName, appDisplayName, resourceTenantId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count > 10` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Multi-Tenant App Access Anomalies** ‚Äî Unexpected tenants or guest users accessing multi-tenant apps may indicate consent phishing or lateral SaaS movement.

Documented **Data sources**: Entra sign-in logs (`resourceTenantId`, `crossTenantAccessType`, `homeTenantId`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with Entra ID sign-in and audit logs in the Microsoft Entra or Azure portal for the same users, resources, and time window.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (cross-tenant access), Heatmap (user √ó tenant), Line chart (volume).
grandmaExplanation: We use identity and sign-in data in Splunk so we can notice unusual logins, access changes, and privileged use while it still matters ‚Äî Multi-Tenant App Access Anomalies
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Authentication.Authentication` dataset once identity and sign-in data are CIM-tagged; otherwise `cimSpl` tstats may return no rows.
